nodes.description: Most STL1 mutations cause premature termination codons leading to nonsense- mediated decay and haploinsufficiency of type II collagen. This results in quantitative rather than qualitative collagen deficiency, producing a milder phenotype than dominant-negative mutations. Tissues with high type II collagen content (vitreous, cartilage, inner ear) are primarily affected.

evidence.reference_title: Stickler syndrome caused by COL2A1 mutations: genotype-phenotype correlation in a series of 100 patients.
evidence.snippet: Stickler syndrome type 1 is predominantly caused by loss-of-function mutations in the COL2A1 gene as >90% of the mutations were predicted to result in nonsense-mediated decay.

evidence.reference_title: Variation in the vitreous phenotype of Stickler syndrome can be caused by different amino acid substitutions in the X position of the type II collagen Gly-X-Y triple helix.
evidence.snippet: Premature termination codons in COL2A1 that result in haploinsufficiency of type II collagen are a common finding.

nodes.description: Type II collagen is essential for vitreous structure. Reduced collagen leads to vitreous liquefaction with characteristic membranous remnants visible on slit-lamp examination, posterior vitreous detachment, and increased retinal detachment risk.

evidence.reference_title: Variation in the vitreous phenotype of Stickler syndrome can be caused by different amino acid substitutions in the X position of the type II collagen Gly-X-Y triple helix.
evidence.snippet: Premature termination codons in COL2A1 that result in haploinsufficiency of type II collagen are a common finding. These produce a characteristic congenital "membranous" anomaly of the vitreous of all affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Variation in the vitreous phenotype of Stickler syndrome can be caused by different amino acid substitutions in the X position of the type II collagen Gly-X-Y triple helix.
evidence.snippet: Experience has shown that vitreous slit-lamp biomicroscopy can distinguish between patients with COL2A1 mutations and those with dominant negative mutations in COL11A1, who produce a different "beaded" vitreous phenotype.
